mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05, or Rank IC > 0.05 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and housing demand, driving negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% annualized during inflationary periods signals capacity constraints that force monetary tightening, resulting in negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth > 2% during CPIAUCSL > 3% yields positive 20-day SPX returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly SPX returns are negative in > 50% of months where PAYEMS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, borrowing costs severely constrain corporate leverage and consumer spending, causing immediate negative equity momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in > 65% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly real consumption expenditures reveals unsustainable economic expansion, triggering a monthly equity correction as growth expectations reset.
failure_condition: Positive GDP-consumption divergence consistently predicts positive 20-day SPX returns with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and near-term corporate earnings momentum, driving positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no statistical relationship with INDPRO momentum, or mean returns are negative during production expansions.
